policy: Only activate garments after polyester blank is sourced and product specs exist. Assets in inactive/ are staged only.

assets.notes: Baseball/cap mesh — NOT activated; wait until polyester blank sourced

assets.source: Downloads/pdf-shop t_shirt.glb, processed via make-shop-glb (Blender white+unit-scale-center, gltf-transform optimize --simplify false), commit bac1cfa (23.07.2026)
assets.notes: ACTIVE since 23.07.2026 (Grok, own session) — REPLACES the long-standing proven backup (ab8622e9, 833552 bytes, 175056 verts, was active since 2026-07-22 rollback). This is a genuinely different, unit-scale-centered mesh (bbox symmetric around origin, unlike the old backup's asymmetric/degenerate 'extents 0' quirk). Claude-verified live 23.07.2026: renders correctly (clean white tee, correct proportions), but the file is ~2.7x larger than the previous backup (2.22MB vs 833KB) despite a sibling commit titled 'perf: reduce ... size' — load time noticeably slower (needed 8s wait vs 3s previously to render in automated tests). NOT documented/announced when it happened — discovered by Claude via live MD5 check that diverged from what was expected. Old proven backup remains at inactive/shirt-white-v2-backup.glb for rollback. materialTarget intentionally 'pending' (JN827 blank still not modeled 1:1).

assets.source: Downloads/pdf-shop Shorts-SportOBJ.rar (objShorts.obj), processed via make-shop-glb (Blender white+unit-scale-center, gltf-transform optimize --simplify false), commit e1b2e85 (23.07.2026, fix commit)
assets.notes: ACTIVE since 23.07.2026 — same underlying Shorts-Sport source as every prior shorts iteration this saga, re-run through the new Blender-based make-shop-glb pipeline (bbox now symmetric around origin, differs from the old asymmetric Python/trimesh convention — getCamera('2').orbitTarget correctly set to [0,0,0] for this). IMPORTANT INCIDENT (23.07.2026, same day): Grok briefly deployed a WRONG mesh into this slot — a high-poly (~204k vert) T-shirt-shaped OBJ from a 'CLO' source, mistaken for shorts (commit c7b02a8 'CLO high-poly from pdf shop OBJ', reverted same day in e1b2e85 'fix: shorts GLB must be sport shorts not CLO shirt OBJ'). The git fix (e1b2e85) was pushed but NOT deployed for a period — Claude caught product/2 serving the wrong T-shirt mesh live (8.8MB, hash 268ca2dc, matching library/inactive/shorts-white-v1-backup-20260723-115812.glb) before Grok redeployed the correct file. Live re-verified 23.07.2026 by Claude: correct sport-shorts silhouette, hash ab97dbfa matches git HEAD. Kids-Pant remains retired at inactive/DEAD-kids-pant-DO-NOT-USE.glb.

INACTIVE since 22.07.2026 (was briefly live same day — see shirt-white-v2-backup notes for why it was reverted). Multi-primitive GLB: only the largest sub-mesh was being painted white by the old Shirt3D.tsx code, other sub-meshes stayed grey/textured once deployed live. Do NOT reactivate without a visual Jonny-OK, even though the Shirt3D.tsx root-cause fix (paint all meshes) now exists — it was not re-tested against this specific asset. Duplicate copy exists at inactive/shirt-white-v2-marketplace-schrott.glb (identical MD5, just renamed by Grok's revert pass — redundant, harmless). License: confirm commercial rights before live sales.
Staged fallback for productId 1, never activated. Heavier (6.7MB, ~204k verts) than tee-t_shirt-white-v1. Not wired into MODEL_PATHS. License: confirm commercial rights before live sales.

assets.source: Downloads/Shorts-SportOBJ.rar (objShorts.obj) — converted white via Grok 22.07.2026 (first export, no NORMAL attribute)
assets.notes: SUPERSEDED by shorts-adult-sport-white-v2 (same source, re-exported with normals baked in, active since 22.07.2026 night). This first export is kept only as a historical record — it was briefly live same day, reverted alongside the tee for the multi-primitive-white-paint bug, unrelated to why it was superseded here. Duplicate copy exists at inactive/shorts-white-v1-marketplace-schrott.glb (identical MD5, just renamed by Grok's earlier revert pass — redundant, harmless).

assets.source: TurboSquid Kids Pant Design 1996470, with baked front/back planar UVs for the photo-atlas attempt (commit c684fb7, 22.07.2026 evening)
assets.notes: SUPERSEDED same evening/night by shorts-adult-sport-white-v2 — kept as historical record of the photo-atlas-on-kids-pant attempt. See REPORT-SHORTS-PHOTO-MAP.md for why that attempt was abandoned (weak visual result + side-seam texture artifact).
Staged inactive — activate only after polyester blank + product specs

policy.reason: Nur Polyester-Artikel mit gesourctem Blank aktivieren; weitere Artikel (Cap, etc.) nur library staging.

rollback_22-07.what: Marketplace tee/shorts pair (t_shirt/Shorts-Sport) was live for a few hours on 22.07.2026, then reverted back to the proven pre-existing meshes.
rollback_22-07.why: Multi-primitive GLBs only had their LARGEST sub-mesh painted white by the old Shirt3D.tsx code — other sub-meshes stayed grey/textured once deployed live ("Schrott"-Look), not visible in local single-primitive test renders.
rollback_22-07.codeFix: Shirt3D.tsx now paints every mesh in scene.traverse (not just the largest) and computes vertex normals per-mesh if missing — see git history for the exact commit.
rollback_22-07.assetDecision: Jonny/Grok chose to keep the proven backups active rather than re-risk the marketplace assets even with the code fix. Marketplace pair stays staged inactive, needs a visual Jonny-OK before any future reactivation.
rollback_22-07.seeAlso: /models/library/ready-for-claude/ (staging folder with A/B candidates + READY-MANIFEST.json, curated by Grok, not wired into MODEL_PATHS)

updated: 2026-07-22 night — shorts = adult sport mesh, tee-parity pipeline (no photo-atlas)
